series.title: DMS Plan
series.subtitle: Data management, workflow design, and repository delivery
series.description: A series for operational DMS planning, data workflow design, and the path from project systems to repository-ready delivery.

entries.title: DMS Plan Overview
entries.subtitle: Data management, workflow design, and repository delivery
entries.summary: Overview entry for the DMS Plan series and its operational workflow materials.

entries.title: Data Management and Sharing Plan
entries.subtitle: From project systems to repository delivery
entries.summary: An operational workflow entry showing how DMS planning becomes real work from source systems to structured data and repository delivery, with a live Builder-plus-review service that helps teams start, update, and review plans.

series.title: Sutton SignWriting Evidence and Adoption
series.subtitle: Documented use, institutional anchors, lineages, and empirical anchors for SignWriting in practice
series.description: The empirical evidence and adoption series for Sutton SignWriting: documented use, institutional anchors, country evidence, and evidence boundaries that show where SignWriting is real in practice while keeping outcome and scale claims bounded.
series.creator: Steve Slevinski

entries.title: Sutton SignWriting Evidence and Adoption Overview
entries.subtitle: Documented use, institutional anchors, and evidence boundaries
entries.summary: Documented use, institutional anchors, and evidence boundaries show where SignWriting is already visible and where empirical claims must remain bounded.

entries.title: Brazil as the Empirical Center of SignWriting
entries.subtitle: Documented institutional density, research opportunity, and why Brazil matters
entries.summary: Brazil is the clearest documented empirical center for SignWriting use, research, and institutional density.

entries.title: South Korea as a Current Institutional Case Study
entries.subtitle: National dictionary publication, current institutional use, and why Korea matters
entries.summary: South Korea shows SignWriting functioning inside current public dictionary infrastructure connected to a national-language institution.

entries.title: Evidence Boundaries and Adoption Limits
entries.subtitle: What current evidence supports, what remains open, and what resistance still needs documentation
entries.summary: Current SignWriting evidence supports documented use and institutional anchors, while outcome claims, adoption density, resistance, and negative cases remain bounded research questions.

entries.title: What the Current Evidence Base Shows
entries.subtitle: Strong evidence, partial evidence, and how to read SignWriting adoption claims
entries.summary: A synthesis brief distinguishing strong, partial, and still-open evidence for SignWriting adoption and infrastructure claims.

series.title: Formal SignWriting
series.subtitle: Plain-text encodings, grammar, search, layout, rendering, styling, authority, and implementation of written sign text
series.description: The technical text-model series for Formal SignWriting: FSW, SWU, signboxes, grammar, search, layout, rendering, styling, implementation practice, and the boundary between computational validity and linguistic judgment.
series.creator: Steve Slevinski

entries.title: Formal SignWriting Overview
entries.subtitle: Plain-text encodings, grammar, search, rendering, styling, and implementation
entries.summary: Plain-text encodings, grammar, search, layout, rendering, styling, and implementation practice define the Formal SignWriting technical model.

entries.title: Formal SignWriting
entries.subtitle: The two-part word, sign text, and the core model
entries.summary: A Formal SignWriting word combines a spatial signbox with an optional temporal prefix so sign text can be stored, searched, checked, and processed.

entries.title: FSW and SWU
entries.subtitle: Plain-text encodings, character sets, and conversion
entries.summary: Plain-text encodings, explicit character inventories, conversion, and canonical text boundaries.

entries.title: The Shape of a Sign
entries.subtitle: Symbols, coordinates, centering, and the signbox
entries.summary: Symbols, coordinates, centering, and signbox structure define the spatial layer of a Formal SignWriting word.

entries.title: Grammar of Formal SignWriting
entries.subtitle: Token patterns, syntax, and valid structure
entries.summary: Token patterns, syntax, regular expressions, and well-formed Formal SignWriting text.

entries.title: Searching Signed Text
entries.subtitle: Query language, matching, ranges, variance, and search logic
entries.summary: Query language, matching, ranges, variance, and signed-text search define the searchable layer of Formal SignWriting.

entries.title: Sign Text Layout
entries.subtitle: Vertical writing, horizontal writing, lanes, centering, and reading levels
entries.summary: Vertical and horizontal writing, lanes, centering strategy, and SW1-SW4 reading-level defaults define sign text layout.

entries.title: Rendering Formal SignWriting
entries.subtitle: SVG, fonts, layout, and output models
entries.summary: SVG, fonts, layout, and output models belong to the rendering layer rather than canonical text.

entries.title: Styling Signs
entries.subtitle: The style string as light markup for customization and rich presentation
entries.summary: The style string as optional light markup for customization and rich presentation.

entries.title: Formal SignWriting in Practice
entries.subtitle: Libraries, datasets, transformations, and implementation pathways
entries.summary: Libraries, datasets, transformations, and implementation pathways connect Formal SignWriting to real software and data practice.

entries.title: Encoding Stewardship and Stability
entries.subtitle: Technical coherence, computational validity, linguistic judgment, practical finality, and forkability
entries.summary: A governance note explaining that FSW/SWU are stable author-stewarded encodings for computational validity, not claims of linguistic or community authority.

series.title: Sutton SignWriting Foundations
series.subtitle: Language, literacy, writing, and the core conceptual argument for SignWriting
series.description: The conceptual foundation for Sutton SignWriting: language is not reducible to sound, writing is not inherently linear, and signed languages need durable read-write pathways of their own.
series.creator: Steve Slevinski

entries.title: Start Here
entries.subtitle: What Sutton SignWriting is, why it matters, and where to go next
entries.summary: Plain-language orientation to Sutton SignWriting, the Sutton SignWriting Foundations series, and the core public argument.

entries.title: Language Without Sound
entries.subtitle: Brain networks, pathway expansion, literacy, and the case for SignWriting infrastructure
entries.summary: Language is not reducible to sound, literacy is a durable symbolic layer, and SignWriting offers a native-facing written pathway for signed languages.

entries.title: The Shape of Writing
entries.subtitle: Why signed writing does not need to fit into a line
entries.summary: Writing is not inherently linear, and SignWriting is a strong case of plane-based writing.

series.title: Sutton SignWriting Infrastructure and Program
series.subtitle: Continuity, stewardship structure, AI/data governance, and the practical program behind the Sutton SignWriting platform
series.description: The stewardship and program-design series for Sutton SignWriting: existing infrastructure, continuity risks, host requirements, international participation, support models, AI/data governance, and operational stewardship.
series.creator: Steve Slevinski

entries.title: Sutton SignWriting Infrastructure and Program Overview
entries.subtitle: Continuity, stewardship, host structure, and program design
entries.summary: Continuity, stewardship functions, host structure, international coordination, and program design define the infrastructure question for Sutton SignWriting.

entries.title: Executive Brief
entries.subtitle: Sutton SignWriting as research infrastructure
entries.summary: Sutton SignWriting is existing research infrastructure with real assets, an unmet continuity layer, and a clear first-stage stewardship opportunity.

entries.title: Infrastructure and Stewardship
entries.subtitle: Confirmed assets, current risks, and the case for stronger stewardship structure
entries.summary: Sutton SignWriting has real infrastructure assets, active stewardship, and continuity risks that justify a stronger accountable structure.

entries.title: International Shared Support
entries.subtitle: Institutional backing, international participation, and shared continuity for Sutton SignWriting
entries.summary: A shared stewardship model for sustaining Sutton SignWriting as international language infrastructure rather than a single-maintainer project.

entries.title: Country Ambassadors
entries.subtitle: Country-level outreach, translation, and local relationship-building
entries.summary: A volunteer-first ambassador model for local outreach, translation, relationship-building, and support documentation.

entries.title: Host Structure and Stewardship Requirements
entries.subtitle: Functions a durable stewardship structure would need to provide
entries.summary: A university, lab, institute, nonprofit, consortium, or fiscal sponsor would need named stewardship functions to support Sutton SignWriting responsibly.

entries.title: Infrastructure Core and Philanthropy
entries.subtitle: A stewardship model for staffing, translation, coordination, and continuity
entries.summary: A focused SignWriting infrastructure core could stabilize existing tools, standards, data, translation, coordination, and international access work.

entries.title: Relevant Precedents for SignWriting Infrastructure
entries.subtitle: What comparable archive, language, data, and research-infrastructure models suggest
entries.summary: SignWriting follows a recognizable infrastructure path: mature assets first, institutional anchoring second, staffing and continuity next, and grants or philanthropy after that.

entries.title: SignWriting, AI, and Layered Stewardship
entries.subtitle: Written signs, records, media, datasets, encodings, and deployment
entries.summary: A position note for sign-language AI governance that separates reusable written signs from authored passages, curated records, media, datasets, encodings, and AI deployment.

series.title: Sutton SignWriting Language and Literacy Research
series.subtitle: Developmental pathways, literacy layering, reading questions, and research-program design
series.description: The language and literacy research-program series for Sutton SignWriting: developmental pathways, literacy layering, reading signed text, study design, materials, corpora, and outcome-study needs.
series.creator: Steve Slevinski

entries.title: Sutton SignWriting Language and Literacy Research Overview
entries.subtitle: Developmental pathways, literacy layering, reading questions, and signed-language research
entries.summary: Language pathways, literacy layering, reading questions, and study design define the research agenda opened by signed-language writing.

entries.title: The Four Language Pathways
entries.subtitle: A developmental model of language, literacy, and signed-language writing
entries.summary: Language input, language output, literacy input, and literacy output create a research-facing model for signed-language writing.

entries.title: Reading Signed Text
entries.subtitle: Recognition, syntax, and spatial reference in signed-language literacy
entries.summary: Recognition, syntax, spatial reference, layout direction, and evidence limits shape the research question of reading signed text.

entries.title: Research Program for Literacy Without Sound
entries.subtitle: Study design, materials, corpora, and the next empirical steps for signed-language literacy
entries.summary: A SignWriting-centered research program can begin with early studies, materials work, corpora, and reusable research infrastructure.

series.title: Notebook
series.subtitle: Working notes, conceptual fragments, and exploratory material that is valuable but not yet settled
series.description: A low-prominence series for exploratory SignWriting notes, pathway sketches, conceptual fragments, and material that may later graduate into stronger public series.

entries.title: Notebook Overview
entries.subtitle: Working notes, conceptual fragments, and exploratory material
entries.summary: Overview entry for the low-prominence working-notes series.

entries.title: Opening Note
entries.subtitle: Why this notebook exists and what belongs here
entries.summary: Opening entry for the Notebook series.

series.title: Signed Language Writing Critical Review Series
series.subtitle: Writing systems, text infrastructure, and critical evaluation
series.description: The critical-method and review series for signed-language writing: a full-path framework and structured reviews that evaluate scholarship, standards, tools, datasets, and infrastructure claims at the layer they actually address and against the problem they actually claim to solve.
series.creator: Steve Slevinski

entries.title: Signed Language Writing Critical Review Series Overview
entries.subtitle: Scope, method, structure, reading paths, and future direction for the review series
entries.summary: Full-path review of signed-language writing, notation, encoding, standards, tools, datasets, and infrastructure claims.

entries.title: Full-Path Critique Framework for Signed-Language Writing and Text Infrastructure
entries.subtitle: A methodological guide for evaluating writing systems, notation systems, encodings, standards, tools, datasets, research proposals, and infrastructure claims
entries.summary: A reusable evaluation framework for signed-language writing systems, text infrastructure, encodings, standards, tools, datasets, and adjacent-purpose systems.

entries.title: Sutton SignWriting and Formal SignWriting Through the Framework
entries.subtitle: A self-application of the review method to the writing system and its technical model
entries.summary: A self-application distinguishing Sutton SignWriting as a human writing system from Formal SignWriting as its technical text model.

entries.title: A Layer-Based Evaluation of Signed Language Writing Systems
entries.subtitle: A comparative demonstration across Filhol, Grushkin, Thiessen, and Unicode SignWriting
entries.summary: A bounded four-case comparison showing how layer-level and full-path judgments clarify signed language writing, encoding, and standards claims.

entries.title: A Review of Official Unicode SignWriting
entries.subtitle: The Character Problem and the Written-Sign Problem
entries.summary: Official Unicode SignWriting is useful as character standardization but incomplete as a compatible written-sign production path.

entries.title: A Review of Grushkin (2017)
entries.subtitle: Writing Signed Languages: What For? What Form? and the question of signed-language writing purpose
entries.summary: Grushkin is correct and useful on the need for signed writing, but not full-path adequate as an alphabetic prescription.

entries.title: A Review of Filhol (2020)
entries.subtitle: A Human-Editable Sign Language Representation and the limits of representation as writing
entries.summary: Filhol is useful as semantic and software representation, but misleading when overextended into a writing-system replacement claim.

entries.title: A Review of Thiessen (2011)
entries.subtitle: A Grammar of SignWriting and the boundary between writing practice and linguistic description
entries.summary: Thiessen is correct and useful as script-internal description, but structurally incomplete where symbols remain too central.

entries.title: A Review of Martin (2000)
entries.subtitle: From Notation to Writing and the threshold between descriptive systems and written text
entries.summary: Martin is correct and useful as an early SignWriting-positive comparison, but evidence-limited as a full-path claim.

entries.title: A Review of Meletis and Dürscheid (2022)
entries.subtitle: Writing Systems and Their Use and the typological limit of signed language writing
entries.summary: Meletis and Dürscheid are strong at general grapholinguistics but typologically incomplete for signed-language writing.

entries.title: From Critique to Construction: Priorities for Signed-Language Writing Infrastructure
entries.subtitle: A constructive synthesis for research, tooling, standards, and adoption
entries.summary: A forward-looking synthesis that turns critique toward evidence, tools, corpora, interoperability, data governance, and stewardship.

series.title: Unicode and SignWriting
series.subtitle: Compatibility, chronology, developer guidance, and standards-facing technical notes
series.description: The standards and compatibility series for Unicode and SignWriting: Unicode chronology, official encoding status, production-compatibility judgments, developer guidance, Wikimedia context, and UTC-facing technical notes.
series.creator: Steve Slevinski

entries.title: Unicode and SignWriting Overview
entries.subtitle: Unicode history, compatibility, and standards-facing discussion
entries.summary: Unicode history, current production encodings, compatibility limits, developer guidance, Wikimedia context, and standards-facing next steps belong in one technical record.

entries.title: Unicode Overview
entries.subtitle: Current position and why the issue keeps returning
entries.summary: Short baseline explanation of why character naming, stable symbols, and written signs remain different questions for SignWriting.

entries.title: Developer Notes
entries.subtitle: Production encoding guidance and compatibility cautions
entries.summary: Implementation guidance for developers working with Sutton SignWriting data, conversion, symbol identity, and Unicode-facing workflows.

entries.title: Chronology and Record
entries.subtitle: Official submissions, minutes, and related documents
entries.summary: A dated record of the main Unicode and related SignWriting documents relevant to current compatibility and standards discussions.

entries.title: Wikimedia Brief
entries.subtitle: What Wikimedia needs to understand about Unicode and SignWriting
entries.summary: A short practical note for Wikimedia staff and collaborators about why naming characters is not enough for current SignWriting project needs.

entries.title: Draft UTC-Facing Technical Note
entries.subtitle: Technical note on compatibility, symbol identity, spatial composition, and next steps
entries.summary: Official Unicode SignWriting can name much of the character layer, but it does not preserve stable symbol identity or provide compatible written-sign support.

series.title: University of Iowa
series.subtitle: University-facing outreach and collaboration materials
series.description: A public Iowa-facing collaboration packet for practical research data support, PI or lab base conversations, and longer-term Sutton SignWriting infrastructure alignment.

entries.title: University of Iowa Overview
entries.subtitle: Practical research support, PI or lab base conversations, and infrastructure alignment
entries.summary: Overview entry for Iowa-facing collaboration around practical research data support, a possible PI or lab base, and longer-term Sutton SignWriting infrastructure alignment.

entries.title: Research Team Value
entries.subtitle: Practical DMS Plan, workflow implementation, and repository delivery value for the University of Iowa
entries.summary: A University of Iowa-facing entry focused on Steve Slevinski's immediate operational value through DMS Plan support, workflow implementation, code, data shaping, validation, documentation, and repository delivery.

entries.title: PI or Lab Base
entries.subtitle: A practical base for project support, grants, and infrastructure
entries.summary: A PI-facing entry arguing that a PI or lab base now is the small first decision that makes project support, sustained research capacity, grant writing, and future infrastructure more plausible.

entries.title: Outreach Memo
entries.subtitle: A concise Iowa summary for first conversations
entries.summary: A short University of Iowa-facing memo for internal forwarding that summarizes Steve's immediate research value, the PI or lab base opportunity, and the longer infrastructure upside.

series.title: Writing and Representation in Sutton SignWriting
series.subtitle: Plane-based writing, authored space, perspective, and the conceptual place of SignWriting as writing
series.description: The writing-system theory series for SignWriting: plane-based composition, authored space, iconicity, convention, legibility, handwriting, machine encoding, kinematography, and broader typological questions.
series.creator: Steve Slevinski

entries.title: Writing and Representation in Sutton SignWriting Overview
entries.subtitle: Plane-based writing, authored space, representation, and writing-systems questions
entries.summary: Plane-based composition, authored space, convention, handwriting, machine encoding, and typology define the writing-systems question for SignWriting.

entries.title: Plane-Based Writing and Script Typology
entries.subtitle: Where SignWriting fits, and where existing writing-system categories strain
entries.summary: SignWriting is best understood through a two-axis typology: what writing represents and how the written unit is organized for reading.

companion_documents.label: Point, Line, and Plane Evidence Notes

entries.title: Perspective and Authored Space
entries.subtitle: Viewpoint, placement, and the authored space of signed writing
entries.summary: Authored viewpoint, spatial relation, symbol placement, and passage orientation are part of the writing problem rather than secondary visual detail.

entries.title: Iconicity, Convention, and Legibility
entries.subtitle: How motivated visual form becomes disciplined writing
entries.summary: Visual motivation, convention, and legibility show how iconic resources become disciplined writing in SignWriting.

entries.title: Handwriting and Spatial Script Practice
entries.subtitle: Block printing, shorthand, cursive forms, and ordinary signed writing
entries.summary: Handwriting, abbreviation, learner forms, correction, reuse, and material habits show how SignWriting functions as ordinary spatial script practice.

entries.title: Human Writing and Machine Encoding
entries.subtitle: Authorship, serialization, and the layers of signed text
entries.summary: Human signed writing and machine encoding occupy different layers: serialization supports software without making plane-based writing linear.

entries.title: Kinematography and the Limits of Writing-System Typology
entries.subtitle: Signed writing as a pressure test for grapholinguistics
entries.summary: Kinematography tests the limits of current writing-system typology by naming durable inscription of articulatory-spatial-kinetic form.

entries.title: Other Sign Language Scripts and Their Conceptions
entries.subtitle: ASL-phabet, HamNoSys, ASLwrite, and what they imply about written sign language
entries.summary: ASL-phabet, HamNoSys, ASLwrite, and SignWriting show different conceptions of signed-language writing, notation, and representation.
